rulings.Case Title: TONY CHI HUA VS. DAMES SHOUMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partial Release Of Rent Payments Deposited With The Court
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT DAMES SHOUMAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PARTIAL RELEASE OF RENT PAYMENTS DEPOSITED WITH THE COURT is DENIED without prejudice. Defendant fails to explain which equitable considerations he is asking the Court to consider and does not provide any authorities explaining or supporting those considerations. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: POL PLA I CONESA VS. UNION HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 3. PLAINTIFF POL PLA CONESA NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff fails to show that he faces imminent irreparable harm from the condition in the condominium unit or that he lacks an effective remedy at law. Furthermore, Plaintiff has failed to attach a proposed order with their moving papers that would allow the Court to discern his exact requested relief. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. TODD JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Todd Johnson'S Notice Of Motion For Attorney'S Fees; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT TODD JOHNSON DEFENDANT TODD JOHNSON'S NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF is GRANTED as requested. Defendant is the prevailing party in this action and is entitled to recovery of fees per the rental agreement. The hourly rates and the hours claimed are reasonable and necessary. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Cross-Complaint was filed 5 days after the general civil cause of action was dismissed. As was evident from the Court's November 8, 2023 ruling, the sole reason for referral of this action to the general civil track was the dismissed cause of action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S November 15, 2023, Order Or In The Alternative To Have The Case Redesignated As Summary "Cud" Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 15, 2023, ORDER OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO HAVE THE CASE REDESIGNATED AS SUMMARY "CUD" PROCEEDINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion for reconsideration is DENIED. Motion to re-designate the case as a summary CUD proceeding is GRANTED. The Court notes that the circumstances leading to this motion could have been avoided had Plaintiff not included a cause of action for breach of contract in its complaint for unlawful detainer or had Plaintiff dismissed this cause of action at any time between the issuance of the tentative ruling on November 7, 2023 (alerting the Plaintiff to the case management issue resulting from the filing of the combined complaint that included causes of action managed by different departments based on different procedures; and that the Court tentatively decided to preserve all of Plaintiff's claims by setting the matter on the only appropriate case management path for all of those claims) and 9:30 a.m. on November 8, 2023 (the time of the hearing). Plaintiff chose to hold off on actually dismissing the second cause of action until after the hearing. However, as of 1:24 p.m. on November 8, 2023 the cause of action for breach of contact was dismissed resolving the case management issue that gave rise to the transfer. The remaining cause of action is one for unlawful detainer and shall proceed as such. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW LINTON VS. JERIES AZAR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 15, 2023 line 6. PLAINTIFF ANDREW LINTON PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. The Court declines the invitation to reconsider its decision sua sponte. Moving party fails to present any new facts, law or circumstances that could not have been presented at the original hearing or that would have changed the outcome of the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
